name: OpenAI
headline: OpenAI POST-VERDICT DAY-1 CONFIRMATION at 27.5 / Developing. May 18 Musk v. Altman jury verdict (statute-of-limitations dismissal, all claims) is now absorbed into May 18 sub-threshold proposal. Hold lifted; no new May 19 entity-specific evidence. Microsoft deal renegotiation, IPO ~$1T trajectory, PBC structure all carry forward. Compositionally unchanged at 27.5. Next forward signal: Musk appeal filing (~30-day window) + S-1 prospectus disclosure (H2 2026).

ACC.rationale: Sustained. May 18 proposal noted +/- 0.1 offsetting moves on ACC and INT; net composite unchanged. Holding canonical dimensions.

INT.rationale: Sustained. May 18 sub-threshold +0.1 partial-restoration noted but not applied (offsetting -0.1 on ACC).

evidence.finding: May 18 federal jury dismissed all of Musk's claims on statute of limitations grounds in under 2 hours. PBC conversion stands. Musk announced appeal.
Microsoft amended deal: $38B revenue-share cap through 2030, Azure exclusivity dropped, 27% stake preserved.
Brockman $30B stake testimony; family-office entanglement; 'this would make us liars' journal entry — primary-source mission-misalignment admission.

keyEvidence: Verdict procedural (statute of limitations), not merits — PBC conversion stands.
May 18 proposed +/- 0.1 offsetting moves on ACC and INT (no net composite change).
rationale: Confirmation at 27.5 post-verdict. The May 18 proposal documented the verdict and sub-threshold dimensional movement (-0.1 ACC, +0.1 INT) without applying. No new May 19 evidence; carry-forward confirmed. Math: (2.2+2.2+2.3+2.2+2.1+1.8+2.3+1.7)/8 = 16.8/8 = 2.1 → ((2.1-1)/4)*100 = 27.5.
whyHeadline: OpenAI hold lifted post-Musk verdict; carry-forward at 27.5 with IPO trajectory now public.

dominantDimension.note: INT dimension carries the verdict's partial-restoration signal but offset within unchanged composite.

carryForward: Verdict procedural (not merits) — governance restructuring unresolved
Journal entry 'this would make us liars' — primary-source admission
PBC public-benefit obligations under Delaware AG executive-discretion monitor only
boundaryWatchResolution: Not boundary-sensitive. 27.5 sits 6.5pt above Critical/Developing and 13.5pt below Developing/Functional.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
